summary: Do not ship. The locked RED witnesses are sound, but the plan introduces an undeclared DT_UNKNOWN ordering change and its load-bearing production probe remains unrunnable. Simpler alternative: none that satisfies both locked witnesses; a temp-only fix would leave the blob path open. Open question: none.

findings.title: DT_UNKNOWN handling changes snapshot semantics
findings.body: The plan defers `DT_UNKNOWN` resolution until `Entry::is_dir()` processes each entry. Locked Tokio 1.52.3 instead calls `std::fs::DirEntry::file_type().ok()` while filling each read-dir chunk; std performs `fstatat` immediately for `DT_UNKNOWN` and Tokio caches successful results, retrying later only if that eager lookup failed. Deferral therefore observes a later filesystem state. For example, a 64-hex directory replaced by a file after the snapshot is skipped by the baseline’s cached type but processed—and potentially quarantined or collected—by the planned implementation. The reverse replacement can turn a baseline error into an `Ok` skip. This is a second observable behavior change, and W11’s all-graves hook assertion also assumes the missing eager cache.
findings.recommendation: Mirror Tokio exactly in `read_all`: for `DT_UNKNOWN`, eagerly perform the no-follow `fstatat`, cache successful types, convert an initial failure to uncached state, and retry only in `is_dir()`. Add forced-DT_UNKNOWN witnesses covering type replacement and the initial-error retry.

findings.title: The replacement M12 Job still cannot run
findings.body: The proposed Job mounts `files-bulk-ssd`, but the referenced homelab SSOT defines and mounts the production PVC as `files-data`, so this pod remains Pending. The `files` namespace also enforces Restricted Pod Security, while the `python:3-slim` pod lacks `runAsNonRoot`, a nonzero UID/GID, RuntimeDefault seccomp, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, and capability dropping, so admission rejects it first. The purported inline probe remains a placeholder, and cleanup calls `shutil.rmtree` although the shown imports omit `shutil`. Consequently this load-bearing virtiofs gate still cannot produce G1–G6 evidence.
findings.recommendation: Provide the complete homelab-owned Job using `claimName: files-data`, a digest-pinned probe image, Restricted-compliant pod/container security contexts matching the existing deployment, and a complete cleanup-safe script. Require manifest validation or server-side dry-run before treating M12 as executable.

next_steps: Correct and characterize the DT_UNKNOWN snapshot-time behavior.
Replace the M12 pseudomanifest and verify it against the current homelab manifests and admission policy.
